rulings.Case Title: FIRST STUDENT, INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion (1) To Augment The Record And (2) To Compel Deposition Of Orla O?Keeffe
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 18, 2021, Line 2 PETITIONER FIRST STUDENT, INC'S. Motion (1) To Augment The Record And (2) To Compel Deposition Of Orla O?Keeffe Petitioner First Student, Inc.'s motion to augment the record to include extra-record evidence that was not before respondent San Francisco Unified School District when it awarded the challenged contract to Zum Services, Inc. is denied. Petitioner's motion to compel the deposition of Orla O'Keefe is likewise denied. *FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING EMAILED TO ALL COUNSEL* As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: FIRST STUDENT, INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Frith'S Notice Of Motion, Motion, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 18, 2021, Line 2, OTHER MARK FRITH'S Motion To Seal Non-party Mark Frith's motion to seal certain exhibits to the Fearnow Declaration filed on March 10, 2021 in support of Petitioner's supplemental memorandum in support of order to show cause re preliminary injunction is denied. Frith has withdrawn the motion in part as to certain assertedly confidential commercial business information contained Exhibits O through Q. As to the balance, the Court cannot find that the factors set forth in Rule 2.550(d) are satisfied. The motion is supported solely by a brief conclusory declaration of counsel lacking personal knowledge, which is insufficient to meet Frith's burden. (See H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe [the burden of presenting information is "logically placed upon the party seeking the sealing of the documents, who is presumptively in the best position to know what disclosures will harm him and how."].) The mere fact that Frith may have designated a particular document or information as confidential or highly confidential under a protective order, or that the parties may have agreed that it should be so treated, is insufficient. (Cal. R. Ct. 2.551(a) ["The court must not permit a record to be filed under seal based solely on the agreement or stipulation of the parties."]; McNair v. National Collegiate Athletic Association (2015) 234 Cal. App. 4th 25, 35-36 ["the mere agreement of the parties alone is insufficient to constitute an overriding interest to justify sealing the documents"].) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and lists the specific exhibits and page and line numbers of the information to be redacted and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: OAK TRUCKING, LLC. VS. JILS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Liberty Mutual Insurance Companys And The Ohio Casualty Insurance Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Disputed Attorney Work Product And Mediation Confidentiality Claims Related To The Deposition Of Stephen Cole And Document Production Of The Cole Law Firm***Redacted***
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 18, 2021, Line 1, DEFENDANTS LIBERTY MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY AND THE OHIO CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Determination Of Disputed Attorney Work Product And Mediation Confidentiality Claims Related To The Deposition Of Stephen Cole And Document Production Of The Cole Law Firm. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties to appear. Parties to address applicability of Cassel v. Superior Court, 51 Cal.4th 113 (2011) to the facts and legal issues raised by the motion. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With The Judge Pro Tems Order Re Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories Employment And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defendant And Its Counsel, Matthew Yan In The Amount Of 4047.50
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 18, 2021, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MARGARITA HERRERA's Motion To Compel Compliance With The Judge Pro Tems Order Re Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories Employment And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defendant And Its Counsel, Matthew Yan In The Amount Of $4047.50. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties to appear. The subject matter, witnesses and documents which are the focus of the Form Interrogatories in issue is necessary for both parties' trial preparation and settlement discussions. The defendant offered during the meet and confer process to provide further verified Responses to the Form Interrogatories to provide facts, witnesses and communications "most pertinent to the City's defense", which offer plaintiff declined. The Court intends to impose a form of defendant's proposal and fashion an Order defining "most pertinent" with a required level of specificity appropriate to the case at this stage of the litigation. rulings.Case Title: JESUS DIAZ SANCHEZ VS. GALLEHER LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement With Court Reporter Fee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 18, 2021, Line 6 PLAINTIFF JESUS DIAZ SANCHEZ'S Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement With Court Reporter Fee Plaintiff's motion for preliminary approval of class action and PAGA settlement is denied without prejudice to plaintiff refiling the motion on the basis of a proper showing. Although plaintiff's supplemental filings provide marginally more information than their initial moving papers, they remain singularly uninformative as to the relevant factors under Kullar. Thus, counsel's declaration refers to "analysis of that data sampling and rates of non-compliance gathered from the sampling," but without explaining what data was sampled or how, much less what the results of that analysis were. Likewise, the supplemental declaration suffers from the following deficiencies, among others: " It provides estimates of potential class damages on two of the class claims-for overtime and meal and rest breaks-but without mentioning other claims, and without indicating whether the assumptions underlying those estimates (e.g., that every member of the class suffered one violation per workweek) are supported by the data analyzed. " It states that counsel "calculated scenarios on Labor Code penalties starting in the $900,000s," but again fails to disclose what the data sampling showed as to the actual infractions found, or the range of penalties potentially associated with proof of such infractions, or the bases for the various "scenarios" considered; " It discloses that defendants "raised defenses on arbitration agreements that could have precluded moving forward" with the litigation, if defendants successfully moved to compel arbitration, but provides no information as to how many class members entered into such arbitration agreements; " It briefly lists "major discounting factors," but without any individualized discussion or analysis of those factors or of the weight given to any of them. The Court could go on, but the point is the one made in its original tentative ruling: the skimpy discussion in counsel's brief supplemental declaration does not allow the required "understanding of the amount that is in controversy and the realistic range of outcomes of the litigation." (Muñoz v. BCI Coca-Cola Bottling Co. of Los Angeles (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 399, 409.) The motion therefore must be denied without prejudice to plaintiff making a serious effort to satisfy that standard. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: OSCAR CASTRO VS. SWINERTON INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 18, 2021, Line 7, DEFENDANT SWINERTON BUILDERS DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendant Swinerton Builders' demurrer to the first amended complaint ("FAC") is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's demurrer for failure to state a claim is sustained with leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action, and without leave to amend as to the seventh and eighth causes of action. Defendant's demurrer for failure to state a claim is overruled as to the first, second, third, fourth, and fifth causes of action. Defendant's demurrer for uncertainty is overruled in its entirety. *FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING EMAILED TO ALL COUNSEL* As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JOY HOLIDAY ET AL VS. BRIAN E. SORIANO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 18, 2021, Line 7, DEFENDANTS BRIAN SORIANO AND LAW OFFICE OF BRIAN E. SORIANO's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel responses to written discovery served on plaintiffs Joy Holiday, Jessy Lin, Harry Chen and cross-defendant, Joy Express, Inc., is unopposed and granted. Verified responses by plaintiffs/cross-defendant in response to form interrogatories, special interrogatories, and requests for production, shall be served within twenty days of the date of service of a copy of this order with Notice of Entry. All objections to form interrogatories, special interrogatories, requests for production and requests for admission have been waived due to said parties' failure to timely respond. The truth of any matters specified in the requests for admission propounded to plaintiffs and cross-defendants Joy Holiday, Lin and Chen are deemed admitted. Monetary sanction are award in the sum of $3,500.00 to be paid jointly and severally by plaintiffs, Joy Holiday, Jessy Lin, Harry Chen and cross-defendant, Joy Express, Inc. within the aforesaid twenty days. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHAQUILLE STEWART ALEXANDER VS. SAKS & COMPANY LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Specially Appearing Defendant Saks Incorporated Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 18, 2021, Line 10 DEFENDANT SAKS INCORPORATED Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction Saks Incorporated's motion to quash is granted. This motion to quash originally came on for hearing on February 16, 2021. On March 3, 2021, the Court issued an order explaining that plaintiff had not met his burden of demonstrating sufficient minimum contacts for general or specific jurisdiction. (See Ziller Electronics Lab GmbH v. Superior Court (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 1222, 1232-1233; BBA Aviation PLC v. Superior Court (2010) 190 Cal.App.4th 421, 428-429 ["The plaintiff must do more than merely allege jurisdictional facts; plaintiff must provide affidavits and other authenticated documents demonstrating competent evidence of jurisdictional facts."].) The record indicated that Saks Incorporated may be some sort of holding company or had a limited role in processing plaintiff's payroll. There was insufficient evidence that Saks Incorporated pervasively controlled plaintiff's purported employer (Saks & Co. LLC), who has already answered the complaint, or that Saks Incorporated had a role in the employment decisions that form the basis of this case. The Court continued the motion to allow plaintiff to conduct limited jurisdictional discovery. Saks Incorporated's supplemental brief explains that no such discovery occurred and plaintiff has not filed any supplemental briefs or evidence. Therefore, plaintiff still fails to meet his jurisdictional burden and the motion is granted. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for Saks Incorporated is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
